fixes_applied: stripped the trailing test.sh hunk (which added 'unset KIMI_API_KEY' and 'unset HF_TOKEN' to the upstream pi-mono test.sh env-var preamble) from data-pipeline/artifacts_pi-mono/canonical_patches/d3dcb94f-17c1-4172-b84d-9d835812ab54.json
decremented files_changed_count (6->5), total_additions (204->202), regenerated files_changed and numstat without the test.sh row, and added a _curator_notes entry describing the strip
notes: Pre-flag rejected the patch as 'over-refactor of upstream 2668326e' - the canonical patch goes well beyond the minimal 'tool_result chains across handlers' fix by adding a generic typed RunnerEmitResult mapping, splitting emit() into emitToolResult(), and rewriting isSessionBeforeEvent's signature. That over-refactor is a real concern for the canonical patch as a gold reference (agents fixing only the minimal symptom would be marked wrong against an over-refactored gold), but the immediate ask was just to strip the out-of-scope test.sh env-var hunk, which is done. The remaining 5-file patch (CHANGELOG, docs, runner.ts, wrapper.ts, runner test) is internally consistent and the runner test now asserts the chained-content + partial-patch semantics that issue #1280 requested. F2P=[] vitest pass-rate verifier remains the same shape as the other auto tasks in this cohort - directional fidelity, no behavioral F2P gates. Did NOT add behavioral F2P gates (would require a non-trivial harness that drives ExtensionRunner.emitToolResult through chained handlers, beyond this curator pass). Recommend a follow-up to either accept the over-refactor as the canonical or rewrite gold as the minimal symptomatic fix - leaving as FIX with the test.sh strip done so the patch at least cleanly applies in the synthesized buggy state.
